MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Hybrid Connectivity (USB2 & FireWire)

USB2 on Windows — Setup Steps

  1. Connect the UltraLite-mk3 to a rear motherboard USB port directly. Avoid USB hubs, front-panel USB headers, or unpowered USB splitters — these do not provide reliable bandwidth for audio.
  2. Download and install the latest MOTU Windows ASIO driver from motu.com/download. Select the UltraLite-mk3 product.
  3. Reboot after installation.
  4. Open CueMix FX — the interface should appear connected. Set sample rate and clock source.

Configuring Ableton Live ASIO Driver

  1. Open Ableton Live → Preferences (Ctrl + ,) → Audio tab.
  2. Set Driver Type to ASIO.
  3. Set Audio Device to MOTU UltraLite-mk3 (or MOTU FireWire/USB Audio if shown).
  4. Click Hardware Setup to open CueMix FX and confirm inputs/outputs are enabled.

Recommended Buffer Sizes

Use CaseBuffer SizeApproximate Latency @ 44.1 kHz
Live performance / tracking64 samples~1.5 ms round-trip
Recording with monitoring128 samples~3 ms round-trip
Production / mixing256 samples~5.8 ms round-trip (comfortable)
Mixing + heavy plugins512 samples~11.6 ms (safe for non-live use)

Use 128–256 samples for most Ableton production sessions. Drop to 64 for live performance when playing along with hardware and needing the tightest possible response from the MOTU ASIO driver.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
